title: Patient → death (deceased)
notes:
Branch = the Patient deceased[x] dimension that decides whether a `death` row exists and what date it carries. Edge `Patient__death` (mapspec/etl/Patient__death.sql + mapspec/views/Patient__death.view.json).

The stage-1 view extracts a single column `death_dt` from `deceased.ofType(dateTime)` (i.e. deceasedDateTime only — a deceasedBoolean does NOT match `ofType(dateTime)`, so it yields null). Stage-2 SQL then emits AT MOST ONE death row per person and only `WHERE death_dt IS NOT NULL`:
  person_id              = referenceToId(Patient.id)
  death_date             = death_dt::date          (calendar date, offset irrelevant)
  death_datetime         = death_dt::timestamp      (wall-clock kept, timezone OFFSET DROPPED — NOT converted to UTC)
  death_type_concept_id  = 32817 constant           (vocab.concept 32817 'EHR', domain Type Concept, standard 'S')
  cause_concept_id       = NULL                      (SQL emits NULL::integer; Patient has no cause-of-death field)
  cause_source_value     = NULL                      (NULL::varchar)
  cause_source_concept_id= NULL                      (NULL::integer)

IMPORTANT — the SQL emits NULL (not 0) for cause_concept_id / cause_source_concept_id even though edge.json declares constant 0; the row matcher asserts the actually-produced row, so these are asserted NULL (omitted). The `death` table has no surrogate PK — person_id IS the PK — but here person_id is the (asserted) FK to person, written as ref:.

This branch scopes the omop expectation to the `death` table ONLY. The `person` row is produced by a different edge (Patient__person) and is out of scope here; every variant therefore lists either {death:[...]} or {} (and a listed-but-absent `death` table means it must be empty). The Patient resources below keep enough for both edges to run but only death is asserted.

Grounding: variant (a) and (d) are real-Synthea-grounded — deceasedDateTime values and the exact cdm_ours_fhir.death output were read from the live DB (patients 5e861fa8… '2024-08-26T10:06:03+01:00' → death_datetime '2024-08-26 10:06:03', and c55a2ed2… '2005-02-10T07:23:04+00:00' → '2005-02-10 07:23:04'). Variant (b) is grounded by the 100 living Synthea patients that produce 0 death rows. Variant (c) deceasedBoolean=true is a hand-constructed corner case (Synthea emits 0 of these) whose empty expectation is DERIVED from `deceased.ofType(dateTime)` yielding null + `WHERE death_dt IS NOT NULL`.

cases.desc: (a) deceasedDateTime present (offset +01:00) → one death row; offset dropped, wall-clock kept
(d) deceasedDateTime present (offset +00:00, UTC) → one death row; cast is ::timestamp, NOT UTC-converted (here already UTC so identical)
(b) no deceased[x] (living patient) → zero death rows (death_dt null → filtered by WHERE death_dt IS NOT NULL)
(c) deceasedBoolean=true ONLY, no deceasedDateTime → zero death rows (deceased.ofType(dateTime) yields null; death_date is NOT NULL so no row can be created)
